FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Namespace FluentGpu.Pal

Classes

InputEventRing

Drained by the host each frame; the window writes POD events into it (move-coalesced).

SystemParams

OS user-preference parameters the engine honors (WinUI reads them through SystemParametersInfo / the registry). The platform writes them ONCE at startup (Win32: HKCU + SPI reads); headless keeps the defaults for determinism. Read anywhere (controls included) — plain statics, no per-frame cost.

Structs

ImeClause

One clause of the in-flight composition string (UTF-16 char range, local to the composition).

InputEvent

POD input event drained from the host-owned ring once per frame (no C# events across the seam). ScrollDelta (Wheel only) is in DIP, oriented so positive = scroll toward the content end (offset increases). The platform pump converts WM_MOUSEWHEEL notches → DIP and flips the sign there. Button: 0 = left, 1 = right, 2 = middle. Mods is the modifier chord at the time of the event (pump-captured); IsRepeat = keyboard auto-repeat (lParam bit 30); TimestampMs = the platform message time (drives double/triple-click detection in the dispatcher).

PopupWindowDesc

Creation parameters for a popup window. Owner = the owning top-level window (the popup stays above it in z-order and never takes activation); BoundsPx = initial bounds in physical virtual-screen px (may be empty — set real bounds via SetBoundsPx(in RectF) before Show).

TitleBarRegion

One reported titlebar region: a rect in CLIENT DIP (the engine's space) + its non-client classification. Pushed on titlebar relayout only (push-on-change — never per frame). First match wins at hit-test, so callers list interactive islands and buttons BEFORE the catch-all Caption band.

WindowDesc

Composited = the window is composited with per-pixel alpha (WS_EX_NOREDIRECTIONBITMAP) so a DirectComposition swapchain can show the DWM Mica backdrop through transparent pixels. CustomFrame = the engine draws the ENTIRE titlebar (WinUI ExtendsContentIntoTitleBar): the platform strips the OS caption (WM_NCCALCSIZE) but keeps the resize frame/shadow, answers WM_NCHITTEST from the engine-reported TitleBarRegions, and synthesizes pointer input for the engine-drawn caption buttons.

Interfaces

IClipboard

The system-clipboard seam (plain Unicode text, the TextBox cut/copy/paste contract). UI-thread only. SequenceNumber is the OS clipboard epoch (Win32 GetClipboardSequenceNumber) — context menus gate their Paste item on it changing instead of polling content (the ISystemColors versioned-external-store idiom).

IPlatformApp
IPlatformLocale

Versioned external-store-shaped locale seam (modeled on ISystemColors). L9.

IPlatformPopupWindow

A borderless, non-activating top-level popup surface (the PAL seam for WinUI windowed popups / E4 out-of-bounds flyouts; later the substrate for E10 tear-out windows). The host owns rendering into it (its own swapchain on Handle); the popup never owns engine state. All bounds are physical virtual-screen px.

IPlatformTextInput

The per-window IME/text-services seam (Imm32 today; the interface is event-shaped so a TSF/ITextStoreACP implementation can replace it without touching the engine — full in-place TSF is a named hardening item).

IPlatformWindow
ITextInputSink

Receives IME composition events from the platform (the focused editor registers as the sink through the input router). Composition TEXT cannot ride the POD InputEventRing (strings) — the platform invokes the sink directly on the UI thread during the message pump; the typing edge is allowed bounded Gen0.

Enums

ImeClauseKind

How an IME composition clause renders (Imm32 ATTR_* / TSF display attributes): Input = dotted underline (not yet converted), Converted = solid underline, TargetConverted/TargetNotConverted = the clause the candidate window currently targets (thick underline).

InputKind
TitleBarHit

Non-client classification for one engine-reported titlebar rect (engine → WM_NCHITTEST). Client marks an INTERACTIVE ISLAND (search box, back/pane buttons) the engine keeps; Caption is the OS drag-move band; the three buttons get HTMIN/HTMAX/HTCLOSE so Win11 shows the snap-layouts flyout over Max.

WindowState

Window placement, surfaced for custom-titlebar chrome (State).

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
